vulnerability.title: Enable Certmap in SSSD
vulnerability.description: SSSD should be configured to verify the certificate of the user or group. To set this up ensure that section like certmap/testing.test/rule_name is setup in /etc/sssd/sssd.conf. For example [certmap/testing.test/rule_name] matchrule =<SAN>.*EDIPI@mil maprule = (userCertificate;binary={cert!bin}) domains = testing.test
vulnerability.recommendation: Review and remediate: Enable Certmap in SSSD

triage_decision.reason: Enabling certmap in SSSD involves adding a configuration section to /etc/sssd/sssd.conf. This is a standard authentication hardening step that does not modify critical boot, partitioning, or firewall settings. If misconfigured, it may cause authentication issues but can be reverted by restoring the original config.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; Incorrect certmap syntax could cause SSSD authentication failures; Service restart required; temporary interruption of SSSD-dependent logins
